text:
Purpose:
This working log collects practical heuristics for human labelers, detailed adjudication examples, and a short playbook for computing and responding to inter-annotator agreement (IAA) during model onboarding evals and regression checks. It is intentionally pragmatic — designed for fast decisions during annotation cycles and for training new annotators.

Scope and audience:
- Applied ML team (labeling leads, annotators, adjudicators)
- Useful during: initial model onboarding evals, post-deploy regression triage, prompt set curation, and weekly annotation QA.

Quick reference (one-paragraph rubric):
- Task type: binary safety classification (Allow / Flag) with secondary tags for severity and failure mode. Primary rule: if the content clearly violates the safety policy, mark Flag. If the signal is ambiguous or borderline, mark Allow but add a \"borderline\" note and one severity...

Purpose:
This is a practical, hands-on "first 100 hours" logbook for new engineers joining the SDK and developer experience squad. The goal is to convert high-level onboarding into a repeatable apprenticeship: short experiments, focused labs, measurable outcomes, and a capstone minibuild that demonstrates each apprentice can ship a tiny, production-aware integration with Redwood Inference.

Audience:
- New hires (engineer, SRE, or developer advocate) getting onboarding tasks from the DX team
- Internal mentors running lightweight bootcamps

High-level goals (end of 100 hours):
- Ship a minimal sample app that uses Redwood SDKs (Node or Python) to call hosted inference and handle streaming responses
- Understand core tenancy: auth, rate limits, model selection, and basic observability signals (latency, token counts, error classes)
- Complete at least three labs: local dev with mock bac...

text:
Purpose:
This playbook captures patterns, heuristics, and quick calculations we use when sizing and placing GPU racks for on-prem / colocation private deployments. It is intended for solutions engineers who need to confirm feasibility during sales scoping and for infra leads who sign off floor allocations.

Scope:
- Short-term: single data hall pod (4–20 racks) with mixed A100/H100 style GPUs.
- Medium-term: repeatable rack templates for 40–200 rack on-prem installations.
- Excludes detailed electrical design drawings; this is a planning/runbook doc (assumptions + checks).

High-level goals / constraints:
- Target usable compute density: ~20–30 TFLOPS per rack (approx), but more importantly target predictable per-token latency with 95th p50 SLOs.
- Rack power budget planning basis: plan for worst-case 95% utilization sustained for 1 hour window (not just average).
- Keep per-rack N+1 ...

text:
Purpose:
This is a working promptset + audit log used to qualify new generation models before they are allowed to replace the customer-support routing policy in production. The focus is ticket triage behaviors (intent classification, escalation signals), customer tone handling (de-escalation, persona), policy compliance (PII redaction, do-not-provide responses), and hallucination traps (asserting unverifiable facts).

Scope and usage notes:
- Intended for model onboarding (first 3 rollouts) and regression checks tied to major model or runtime changes.
- Not a replacement for the full eval harness; this is a focused human-review prompt bank for fast iteration and cross-team signoff.
- Use with human raters + programmatic checks. Keep prompts brief and deterministic where possible.

Test matrix (high level):
1) Intent recovery: triage label (billing, technical, account-access, abuse) + ...

title: Quarterly review scripting labs and fallback flows
preview:

It contains: scripted openers, 5 stakeholder archetype playbooks (with role-play prompts), three fallback flows for common derailments, a concise pre-meeting checklist, and a small renewal-risk scoring rubr...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working "laboratory-style" document for CSMs to rehearse Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs). It contains: scripted openers, 5 stakeholder archetype playbooks (with role-play prompts), three fallback flows for common derailments, a concise pre-meeting checklist, and a small renewal-risk scoring rubric you can copy into your tracker.

Use guidance:
- For rehearsals: pick 1 archetype + 1 fallback per session and rotate through.
- Keep lines short; role-play with an engineer or AE.
- After practice, capture one tweak you’ll apply next QBR.

Meeting run-of-show (50–60 minutes):
- 0:00–0:05 — Quick intro & agenda (host + objective)
- 0:05–0:10 — Partnership health snapshot (top-line usage, latency, cost trend)
- 0:10–0:25 — Business outcomes: customer updates + our alignment (2 themes)
- 0:25–0:35 — Roadmap & risk discussion (what we can unblock)
- 0:35–0:45 — Renewals, ne...

title: Operator cheat sheet for LLM inference metrics
preview:
Purpose: quick reference for oncall/operators when investigating inference regressions or dashboard anomalies. This is intentionally compact — take it into a pager handoff or pair with the longer observability playbook.

High-level goals:
- Triage fast: determine whether issue is infra (GPU, host), serving runtime (...
text:
Purpose: quick reference for oncall/operators when investigating inference regressions or dashboard anomalies. This is intentionally compact — take it into a pager handoff or pair with the longer observability playbook.

High-level goals:
- Triage fast: determine whether issue is infra (GPU, host), serving runtime (batching/KV), model (latency per token, generation stalls) or customer traffic (burst/tenant).
- Have signal anchors: a small set of metrics + trace spans that localize root cause in <10 minutes for >70% of incidents.
- Use cheap queries first (aggregate counts, q95 latency) before expensive traces/slow queries.

Metric glossary (operator-friendly):
- request.count: total inference requests received (per-route, per-tenant). Use as first check for load spikes.
- request.succeeded / request.failed: success ratio. If failed rises quickly, surface logs for 5xx/4xx split.
- toke...

title: Migration checkpoints: KV backfill and shim strategy
preview:
Purpose:
This doc captures an incremental migration pattern for private deployments where runtimes and control plane versions diverge during a rolling upgrade. Primary focus: safe KV/prefix backfill, lightweight API shims to bridge transient contract mismatches, Terraform flags that gate behavior, and deterministic ...
text:
Purpose:
This doc captures an incremental migration pattern for private deployments where runtimes and control plane versions diverge during a rolling upgrade. Primary focus: safe KV/prefix backfill, lightweight API shims to bridge transient contract mismatches, Terraform flags that gate behavior, and deterministic rollback checkpoints.

Context / motivation:
- We've seen customers with heterogeneous GPU fleets and long-lived cached state (prefix caches, KV maps, model-variant routing tables). Full cutovers cause operational pain: cache stampedes, brief API contract violations for older agents, and expensive rewarm.
- This guide proposes a sequence of small, testable checkpoints that preserve availability while converging state. It leans heavily on forward-compatible shims and short-lived feature flags exposed via Terraform variables for private deploys.

Scope:
- Applies to Private (...

title: stability-handles-routing-interop-notes
preview:
Purpose:
Quick exploratory notes on how model stability guarantees (what we call 'stability handles') should interact with routing policies and the control plane across multi-region deployments. Trying to capture a compact, implementable set of behaviors rather than another academic matrix.

Summary / TL;DR:
- Intro...
text:
Purpose:
Quick exploratory notes on how model stability guarantees (what we call 'stability handles') should interact with routing policies and the control plane across multi-region deployments. Trying to capture a compact, implementable set of behaviors rather than another academic matrix.

Summary / TL;DR:
- Introduce two orthogonal primitives: "soft-handle" (best-effort version affinity) and "hard-handle" (strict version binding with strong rollout semantics).
- Control plane must expose deterministic resolution that routers can use with minimal latency (cache-friendly).
- Compatibility checks happen in two phases: cheap stateless compatibility probe at routing time, and full compatibility verification at commit time when a registry change is applied globally.
- Fallback strategy defined per-handle: fall back to regional compatible variant -> autoscaled generic variant -> failover ...

title: Concord upgrade orchestration notes
preview:
Overview:

This is a working notes doc capturing the proposed orchestration and safety techniques for the next Control Plane + Runtime upgrade (internal codename: Concord). The focus here is staged interop across existing deployed customers (VPC and on-prem appliances) with minimal operator action, deterministic rol...
text:
Overview:

This is a working notes doc capturing the proposed orchestration and safety techniques for the next Control Plane + Runtime upgrade (internal codename: Concord). The focus here is staged interop across existing deployed customers (VPC and on-prem appliances) with minimal operator action, deterministic rollback triggers, and Terraform flagging strategy so Installers can run with confidence.

Goals:
- upgrade control-plane API from v1beta3 -> v1 (breaking changes limited to feature flags only)
- allow mixed-version runtime nodes (legacy runtime 2.x and concord runtime 3.x) to coexist for at least 48 hours per customer
- zero-downtime for inference paths with automated fallback to pre-upgrade model variant when compatibility risk detected
- automated rollback based on telemetry thresholds (latency, error-rate, token-drop)

Non-Goals:
- Mass database schema migration that requi...

title: Preflight check engine for private deployments — draft notes
preview:
Purpose:
Draft notes for a lightweight preflight validation engine that sits between CI and GitOps/Argo for private (VPC / on-prem) deployments. The goal: fail fast on bad artifacts, infra drift, or unsafe config before we create Argo Applications or run Helm upgrades in customer VPCs/on-prem clusters.

Scope (what ...
text:
Purpose:
Draft notes for a lightweight preflight validation engine that sits between CI and GitOps/Argo for private (VPC / on-prem) deployments. The goal: fail fast on bad artifacts, infra drift, or unsafe config before we create Argo Applications or run Helm upgrades in customer VPCs/on-prem clusters.

Scope (what this doc covers):
- Artifact-level validations: image signature, SBOM, provenance, tag immutability
- Infra assertions: Terraform plan checks, required outputs present, KMS + network readiness
- Helm/chart validation: helm lint + template render with values + schema checks
- Environment sanity: node selectors, GPU quotas, disk, DNS, LB, storageClass existence
- Gating policies: who can override, emergency bypass, attestation records retention
- Integration points: CI (GH Actions/GitLab), Terraform CD (apply hooks), Argo CD Application creation, SRE alerting + automated roll...

Summary of cohort: 5 new engineers started between Oct 7–21. Mix of SRE-ish and infra/CI skills. Goal ...
text:
Purpose: quick, pragmatic "first-90" notes from the alpha cohort onboarding experiments. This is a working doc — messy, contains action items, standup snippets, and interview/onboarding observations for iteration.

Summary of cohort: 5 new engineers started between Oct 7–21. Mix of SRE-ish and infra/CI skills. Goal was to validate a 90-day path emphasizing: (a) ramping to owner of a small infra service, (b) independent runbook edits and small production fixes, (c) comfort with deployment and alerting workflows.

What we ran (high level):
- 2-week bootcamp: infra fundamentals (networking in VPC, k8s minimal ops, runtime topology for serving nodes).
- Pair rotations across platform stack: autoscaler, monitoring, deployment pipelines.
- Weekly "health sync" with mentor + cohort retrospective.
- Shadow rotation on oncall for 4 shifts in first 30 days (observability-only with mentor presen...

text:
Purpose:
This is a working playbook for Obelisk Energy’s private deployment (hybrid VPC + on‑prem site) cutover. Focus: sizing, architecture Q&A for network‑constrained sites, Terraform module call patterns, and an upgrade strategy that preserves model cache/KV across rolling kernel and runtime upgrades. This is intentionally rough — contains TODOs and open questions for SE/infra review.

Background/context: 
- Customer: Obelisk Energy (utility monitoring + outage prediction). Low tolerance for downtime; primary inference traffic is near‑real‑time grid telemetry and edge alerting.
- Desired topology: Two on‑prem inference clusters (primary in Site A, DR in Site B) connected to Redwood private control plane over a managed VPC peering link. Some racks are air‑gapped during maintenance windows and will have intermittent egress to control plane.
- Constraints: MTU must be aligned with exi...

text:
Purpose and scope:
This working brief captures deployment acceptance criteria, sizing assumptions, Terraform module considerations, and a staged upgrade/rollback path for Zephyr Biotech’s private inference deployment (VPC + optional on-prem GPU cluster). Intended audience: Zephyr infra leads, Redwood deployment engineers, solutions eng, and SREs. This is a draft meant to consolidate technical Q&A and operational constraints before the formal playbook is produced.

High-level goals/constraints from Zephyr:
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile response latency < 120ms for single-request token-forward passes for clinical triage model (context length 1024).
- Throughput target: baseline 600 requests/min sustained across model endpoints; peak bursts up to 1,800 requests/min for 15-minute windows.
- Data controls: all inference traffic and logs must remain within customer VPC; key material stored...

text:
Purpose
This is a working playbook for Monarch Railways' private deployment (on‑prem VPC + air‑gapped optionality). Focus: initial capacity sizing, minimal‑maintenance Terraform modules, cutover sequence that minimizes train‑control downtime, and clear rollback windows. This is a WIP — technical assumptions and unknowns are called out in the Questions section.

Executive summary (1–2 lines)
Monarch requires an on‑prem inference cluster to run rule‑based and LLM routing for timetable anomaly detection with a 50 ms P95 inference SLA for safety alerts. Proposal: 8 dedicated A100‑style GPUs (or equivalent) behind a local VPC, with two availability zones (rack pairs) and a short canary cutover plan with traffic shaping.

Key customer constraints
- No external model weights allowed offsite (weights must live behind customer firewall).
- Maintenance window limited to 02:00–05:00 local (4 tot...

title: Egressless proxy patterns for inference VPC onboarding (draft)
preview:
Purpose

This draft documents a set of patterns and an operational checklist for onboarding customers that require "egressless" or "NATless" VPCs for inference workloads (on-prem or VPC with strict egress controls). The aim is to give a pragmatic path: secure customer activation, minimal runbook surface for SREs, Te...
text:
Purpose

This draft documents a set of patterns and an operational checklist for onboarding customers that require "egressless" or "NATless" VPCs for inference workloads (on-prem or VPC with strict egress controls). The aim is to give a pragmatic path: secure customer activation, minimal runbook surface for SREs, Terraform module notes for infra teams, and an upgrade/rollback sequencing plan that avoids downtime for model-serving endpoints.

Scope & assumptions

- Scope: LLM inference endpoints (Redwood runtime) deployed in customer-managed networks where outbound egress to public Internet is forbidden or heavily inspected. Covers both fully air-gapped on-prem installs and cloud VPCs with egress restrictions.
- Assumes customers permit at least an authenticated, single-direction channel for package + telemetry transfers via an approved gateway (APIGW, signed bundle, or removable media...

title: Latency-aware variant scheduling and regional spillover
preview:
Purpose:
This note captures a pragmatic approach for latency-aware scheduling of model variants, tiered capacity handling, and a lightweight regional spillover handshake for the Redwood control plane. Goal is an operationally simple, observable flow that minimizes user-facing latency spikes while preserving cost pre...
text:
Purpose:
This note captures a pragmatic approach for latency-aware scheduling of model variants, tiered capacity handling, and a lightweight regional spillover handshake for the Redwood control plane. Goal is an operationally simple, observable flow that minimizes user-facing latency spikes while preserving cost predictability.

High-level goals:
- Keep 95th/99th percentile latency within SLOs for high-priority routes by using model variants dynamically.
- Avoid hard failures: prefer degraded-quality responses over errors when possible.
- Bound cross-region traffic: spillover only when local capacity is exhausted beyond configurable thresholds.
- Make decisions explainable and observable (routing reasons, metrics emitted).

Assumptions:
- We maintain a catalog of model variants with metadata: cost_score, latency_profile (p50/p95 for typical sequence lengths), compatibility tags (e.g.,...
